verdict: Moderate to High risk of AI displacement in the near future.

reasoning_steps.title: Increasing AI adoption in the legal profession
reasoning_steps.body: The use of AI-powered stenography tools has already been demonstrated in some courts, with a high success rate.

reasoning_steps.title: Stenographers' role is becoming more focused on review and editing
reasoning_steps.body: As AI takes over the initial transcription process, stenographers will need to focus on reviewing and editing the transcripts for accuracy.

evidence_ledger.meta: Study demonstrating the potential of AI-powered stenography tools in court settings.
evidence_ledger.contribution: Establishes a high success rate for AI-powered stenography tools in court transcription.

evidence_ledger.meta: Provides information on the job duties and requirements of court stenographers.
evidence_ledger.contribution: Highlights the importance of human judgment and review in the role of a court stenographer.

adjacent_careers.name: Court Reporter
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Court reporters and stenographers often overlap in their duties, but the role of a court reporter is more focused on transcription.

adjacent_careers.name: Document Review Specialist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: The skills required for document review are similar to those required for stenography, making this an adjacent career path.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered stenography tool achieves high accuracy rate in court transcription
recent_developments.impact: The use of AI-powered stenography tools is becoming more widespread, potentially displacing human stenographers.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
